# How to Help Yellow Cannabis Seedlings
by Nebula Haze
Having a problem with your cannabis seedlings? Here’s a quick tutorial to help you identify the problem and get it solved right away so you can get back to growing marijuana.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/02/cannabis-seedling-that-has-been-over-watered.jpg)
**What Causes Cannabis Seedlings to Turn Yellow (or Show Other Symptoms)?**
  * **Poor Watering Practices** – When it comes to seedlings, the most common problem is overwatering. Leaves may turn yellow if roots stay wet for long periods of time (“wet feet”) and may also show a wide variety of other symptoms, spots, and nutrient deficiencies. Less commonly similar symptoms can be caused by underwatering but it’s more rare because small plants don’t drink much. [Learn how to water your cannabis plants](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-often-water-marijuana).
  * **Bad Soil** – If the soil is thick and muddy, your seedlings will grow poorly. [Good cannabis soil](https://www.growweedeasy.com/what-soil-is-good-for-growing-cannabis) drains well and is rich and light.
  * **Not Enough Light** – Seedlings get long and stretchy with big spaces between sets of leaves, often with yellowing. Check out a [list of the best LED grow lights for growing cannabis](https://www.growweedeasy.com/5-best-led-grow-lights-2025-cannabis-yields-speed-bud-quality).
  * **Too Much Light** – Parts of the plant closest to the light are turning yellow or brown. [Learn about light burn](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-light-burn).
  * **Too Hot or Dry** – This can cause leaves to turn into tacos, crinkle up at the edges, and sometimes turn yellow if it gets too bad. Learn about [heat stress](https://www.growweedeasy.com/heat-light-stress) and[ controlling temperatures](https://www.growweedeasy.com/temperature).
  * **Incorrect pH** – If pH is too high or too low the plant may show nitrogen or other deficiencies. [Learn about controlling pH](https://www.growweedeasy.com/ph) to prevent [nutrient deficiencies](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-symptoms-pictures). As long as your plant is getting nutrients and doesn’t appear droopy, 9/10 times the nutrient deficiency is actually being caused by the incorrect ph.
  * **Not Enough Nutrients** (rare) – If growing in soil, it’s very unlikely your seedling will use up all the nutrients in the first few weeks. However, if growing in coco or hydro, it’s important to give seedlings [suitable nutrients](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nutrients) from germination or they will quickly turn yellow from the bottom up.

**Examples of Unhealthy Cannabis Seedlings**
**[Overwatering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/overwatering) – Common cause of droopiness and yellow leaves**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/overwatered-young-plant.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/unhealthy-yellow-seedling-stunted.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/overwatered-too-big-pot.jpg)
Here are more examples of nutrient deficiencies that are actually caused by overwatering.
[​](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/looks-like-deficiency-actually-overwatering.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/example-of-marijuana-overwatered-for-many-days.jpg)
Chronic [overwatering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/overwatering) in [thick soil](https://www.growweedeasy.com/what-soil-is-good-for-growing-cannabis) led to the following yellow leaf symptoms. The main thing that alerts you to the fact that the yellowing is caused by overwatering is the fact that the seedling is droopy. It’s very easy to overwater young plants when you have a small seedling in a big container. When that’s the case, don’t soak the whole growing medium. Water in just a circle around the plant until its leaves are up and growing fast. At that point you can start watering more thoroughly 🙂
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/sick-cannabis-plant-overwatering-too-big-pot.jpg)
The strange yellow and red growth in this picture has been caused by giving the plant too much water, too often. Overwatering can often cause what appears to be nutrient deficiencies in young plants. Older plants tend to just droop, but seedlings often have odd reactions to root problems.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/yellow-and-red-growth-caused-overwatering.jpg)
**Bad Soil –[What Soil is Good for Cannabis?](https://www.growweedeasy.com/what-soil-is-good-for-growing-cannabis)**
This is terrible soil. Good cannabis soil should never appear this shiny and waterlogged no matter how much you water your plants.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/stunted-seedling-overwatered-bad-soil.jpg)
This soil is slightly better, but still thick and thoroughly saturated with water. As a result, the young seedling roots can’t get any oxygen and it’s stopped growing, with the first set of leaves starting to turn yellow. Bad soil will stunt young plants and keep them small, especially when combined with overwatering.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/overwatering-seedling-causes-stripes-and-yellow-leaves.jpg)
This “soil” is more like mud. The plant roots are drowning from lack of oxygen, causing severe wilting.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2011/02/drowning-in-mud.jpg)
**Not Enough Light – Tall or “Stretchy” Seedlings**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/not-enough-light-leggy-seedling.jpg)
Seedlings stay yellow if they never get any light.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/stretchy-seedling-not-enough-light.jpg)
**[Too Much Light](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-light-burn) – parts of plants closest to the light are turning yellow**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/light-burned-leaves.jpg)
**[Heat Stress](https://www.growweedeasy.com/heat-light-stress) – Causes leaves to fold into tacos and edges to turn up**
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/06/suffering-from-heat.jpg)
These two seedlings are suffering partly from overwatering, and partly from heat.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/heat-seedling-curling-down.jpg)
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/root-problems-coco-coir.jpg)
After being subjected to overwatering plus a heat wave, the leaves of the following seedling started cupping upwards and turned lime green. The stems and veins of the leaves were turning red. You can see the soil is still dark and wet because the plant stopped drinking after developing root problems.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/lime-green-root-problems.jpg)
**Not Enough[Nutrients](https://www.growweedeasy.com/nutrients)**
Too-low overall levels of nutrients usually only happens to cannabis seedlings in coco or hydro where the grower didn’t provide any extra nutrients. It’s rare for a young seedling in soil to be able to use up all the nutrients in the soil in the first few weeks. In coco or hydro, you should be providing nutrients (at seedling strength) from the very first watering because otherwise it only has the nutrients that were contained in the seed.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2017/02/seedling-nitrogen-deficiency-hydro-need-nutrients.jpg)
* * *
[More Examples of Common Cannabis Seedling Problems](https://www.growweedeasy.com/seedling-problems)
